Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Add entire.io CLI commands to the TUI (approved)

## Context
The entire.io CLI has commands like `status`, `explain`, `doctor`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, and `clean`. entire-local currently only reads checkpoint data. We want to mirror these commands in the TUI by shelling out to the `entire` CLI binary, making entire-local a full interface for entire.io.

## UX Design: Action Menu

Triggered by pressing `a` (matching the existing pattern of single-key modes like `/` for filter, `?` for help). Shows a centered overlay box (same pattern as `help.go`).

**Menu items:**
```
Actions
─────────────────────────────
 [s]  Status       Show entire.io state
 [e]  Explain      AI analysis of checkpoint
 [d]  Doctor       Diagnose stuck sessions
 [w]  Rewind       Rewind to checkpoint
 [m]  Resume       Resume session on branch
 [x]  Reset        Delete session state
 [c]  Clean        Remove orphaned data
─────────────────────────────
 esc  Close
```

- Checkpoint-specific items (`explain`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`) are dimmed/hidden on the overview screen
- All items disabled with warning when `entire` CLI is not installed
- Navigate with `j/k`, select with `Enter` or shortcut key

## Execution Strategy

| Command | Mode | Confirmation | Notes |
|---------|------|-------------|-------|
| `status` | Capture output, show in overlay | No | `entire status --detailed` |
| `explain` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | `entire explain -c <id>` -- may stream/generate |
| `doctor` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | Interactive (offers fix options) |
| `rewind` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire rewind --to <commit>` -- destructive |
| `resume` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire resume <branch>` -- changes branch |
| `reset` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire reset` -- destructive |
| `clean` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire clean` -- destructive |

- **`tea.ExecProcess`**: Suspends TUI, runs command in real terminal, resumes TUI on exit. Good for interactive/long-running/destructive commands.
- **Capture**: Runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()` in a `tea.Cmd`, shows result in a scrollable overlay. Good for quick read-only output.
- After any write command completes, refresh checkpoint data via `m.loadCheckpoints()`.

## Confirmation Flow

For destructive commands (`rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, `clean`):
1. User selects action from menu
2. Confirmation overlay appears: `"Rewind to checkpoint abc1234? This will reset your working tree. (y/n)"`
3. `y` proceeds, `n`/`Esc` cancels

## Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/actions.go` | Action menu: item definitions, rendering, navigation, `buildEntireArgs()` |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/confirm.go` | Confirmation dialog: rendering, y/n handling |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/command_output.go` | Scrollable overlay for captured command output (used by `status`) |

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `dashboard.go` | Add fields to `model` (`showActions`, `actionCursor`, `entireCLIAvailable`, `confirmAction`, `commandOutput`, `showCommandOutput`). Wire overlays into `Update()` priority chain and `View()`. Add `detectEntireCLI()` to `Init()` via `tea.Batch`. |
| `keys.go` | Add `Actions` (`a`), `Confirm` (`y`), `Deny` (`n`) key bindings |
| `messages.go` | Add `entireCLIDetectedMsg`, `entireCmdFinishedMsg`, `entireCmdOutputMsg` |
| `styles.go` | Add `warningStyle`, `disabledStyle`, `actionSepStyle` |
| `help.go` | Add `a` -> `"Actions menu"` to help bindings |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: New message types and CLI detection
- Add msg types to `messages.go`
- Add `detectEntireCLI() tea.Cmd` using `exec.LookPath("entire")` in a new file `actions.go`
- Wire detection into `model.Init()` with `tea.Batch(m.loadCheckpoints(), detectEntireCLI())`
- Handle `entireCLIDetectedMsg` in `Update()`

### Step 2: Action menu definitions and rendering (`actions.go`)
- Define `entireAction` enum and `actionItem` struct (key, label, desc, requiresCheckpoint, destructive, execProcess)
- Define the 7 action items as a package-level slice
- Render function following `help.go` centered-box pattern
- Navigation: j/k cursor, Enter/shortcut-key select, Esc close
- `buildEntireArgs(action, detail)` maps actions to CLI args

### Step 3: Confirmation dialog (`confirm.go`)
- Render centered box with warning text and `y/n` prompt
- `handleConfirmKeys`: `y` -> execute, `n`/`Esc` -> cancel
- Confirmation messages per action (describing consequence)

### Step 4: Command output overlay (`command_output.go`)
- Scrollable panel for `status` output
- j/k scroll, Esc to close
- Reuses existing scroll patterns from detail view

### Step 5: Wire into dashboard.go
- Add new model fields
- `Update()` priority chain: commandOutput > confirm > actions > filtering > normal
- `View()` overlay priority: commandOutput > confirm > actions > help > normal screen
- Action execution:
  - ExecProcess commands: `tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("entire", args...), callback)`
  - Capture commands: `tea.Cmd` that runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()`
- Post-command: refresh checkpoints, clear state

### Step 6: Keys, styles, help updates
- Add key bindings in `keys.go`
- Add styles in `styles.go` (reuse existing colors: warning=accent/214, disabled=very dim)
- Add `a` action to help screen
- Update footer hints in both overview and detail screens

## Key Patterns to Follow
- `help.go` centered overlay box pattern for action menu, confirm, and output
- `messages.go` simple struct msg types
- `keys.go` `key.NewBinding()` pattern
- No comments inside function bodies
- Warm orange theme (208/214 palette)
- `exec.Command` pattern from `pkg/git/git.go`

## Verification
- `make build && make test && make lint` pass
- Press `a` on overview: see global commands, checkpoint-specific items dimmed
- Press `a` on detail: see all commands enabled
- Select `status`: overlay shows `entire status --detailed` output
- Select `explain`: TUI suspends, `entire explain` runs, TUI resumes
- Select `rewind` on detail: confirmation dialog, then `entire rewind` runs
- When `entire` not installed: all items disabled with warning


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/arvos/.claude/projects/-Users-arvos-Tactic-workspaces-workspace-entire-local-cli-entire-local/17cf695e-a6fa-420f-87d3-794fea032082.jsonl

package dashboard

import "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key"

type keyMap struct {
	Up       key.Binding
	Down     key.Binding
	Enter    key.Binding
	Back     key.Binding
	Quit     key.Binding
	Help     key.Binding
	Refresh  key.Binding
	Filter   key.Binding
	Tab      key.Binding
	ShiftTab key.Binding
	Num1     key.Binding
	Num2     key.Binding
	Num3     key.Binding
}

var keys = keyMap{
	Up: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("up", "k"),
		key.WithHelp("k/up", "move up"),
	),
	Down: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("down", "j"),
		key.WithHelp("j/down", "move down"),
	),
	Enter: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("enter", "l"),
		key.WithHelp("enter/l", "select"),
	),
	Back: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("esc", "h"),
		key.WithHelp("esc/h", "back"),
	),
	Quit: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("q", "ctrl+c"),
		key.WithHelp("q", "quit"),
	),
	Help: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("?"),
		key.WithHelp("?", "help"),
	),
	Refresh: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("r"),
		key.WithHelp("r", "refresh"),
	),
	Filter: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("/"),
		key.WithHelp("/", "filter"),
	),
	Tab: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("tab"),
		key.WithHelp("tab", "next tab"),
	),
	ShiftTab: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("shift+tab"),
		key.WithHelp("shift+tab", "prev tab"),
	),
	Num1: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("1"),
		key.WithHelp("1", "transcript tab"),
	),
	Num2: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("2"),
		key.WithHelp("2", "files tab"),
	),
	Num3: key.NewBinding(
		key.WithKeys("3"),
		key.WithHelp("3", "plan tab"),
	),
}

			BorderForeground(subtle).

			Foreground(subtle)

				Foreground(subtle)

	normalRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			PaddingLeft(3)

	hunkHeaderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(info)

	diffFileHeaderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(subtle).
				Bold(true)
)

    25→	sb.WriteString("\n\n")
    26→
    27→	for _, b := range bindings {
    28→		sb.WriteString("  ")
    29→		sb.WriteString(helpKeyStyle.Render(padRight(b.key, 14)))
    30→		sb.WriteString(helpDescStyle.Render(b.desc))
    31→		sb.WriteString("\n")
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	sb.WriteString("\n")
    35→	sb.WriteString(dimStyle.Render("  Press ? or Esc to close"))
    36→
    37→	content := sb.String()
    38→

    40→	box := lipgloss.NewStyle().
    41→		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
    42→		BorderForeground(primary).
    43→		Padding(1, 2).
    44→		Width(boxWidth).
    45→		Render(content)
    46→
    47→	return lipgloss.Place(width, height,
    48→		lipgloss.Center, lipgloss.Center,
    49→		box,

    54→	if len(s) >= n {

    56→	}

    11→func ExecGit(repoRoot string, args ...string) (string, error) {
    12→	fullArgs := append([]string{"-C", repoRoot}, args...)
    13→	cmd := exec.Command("git", fullArgs...)
    14→	out, err := cmd.Output()
    15→	if err != nil {
    16→		return "", err
    17→	}
    18→	return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
    19→}

    21→func RepoRoot() (string, error) {
    22→	cmd := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel")
    23→	out, err := cmd.Output()
    24→	if err != nil {
    25→		return "", err
    26→	}
    27→	return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
    28→}

    30→func BranchExists(repoRoot, branch string) bool {
    31→	_, err := ExecGit(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+branch)

// Package checkpoint loads and parses entire.io checkpoint data from git.
package checkpoint

import "time"

type TokenUsage struct {
	InputTokens         int `json:"input_tokens"`
	CacheCreationTokens int `json:"cache_creation_tokens"`
	CacheReadTokens     int `json:"cache_read_tokens"`
	OutputTokens        int `json:"output_tokens"`
	APICallCount        int `json:"api_call_count"`
}

type SessionRef struct {
	Metadata    string `json:"metadata"`
	Transcript  string `json:"transcript"`
	Context     string `json:"context"`
	ContentHash string `json:"content_hash"`
	Prompt      string `json:"prompt"`
}

type Attribution struct {
	AgentLines      int     `json:"agent_lines"`
	HumanAdded      int     `json:"human_added"`
	HumanModified   int     `json:"human_modified"`
	TotalCommitted  int     `json:"total_committed"`
	AgentPercentage float64 `json:"agent_percentage"`
}

type Metadata struct {
	CheckpointID     string       `json:"checkpoint_id"`
	CLIVersion       string       `json:"cli_version"`
	Strategy         string       `json:"strategy"`
	Branch           string       `json:"branch"`
	CheckpointsCount int          `json:"checkpoints_count"`
	FilesTouched     []string     `json:"files_touched"`
	Sessions         []SessionRef `json:"sessions"`
	TokenUsage       TokenUsage   `json:"token_usage"`
}

type SessionMetadata struct {
	CheckpointID string      `json:"checkpoint_id"`
	SessionID    string      `json:"session_id"`
	Strategy     string      `json:"strategy"`
	CreatedAt    string      `json:"created_at"`
	Branch       string      `json:"branch"`
	FilesTouched []string    `json:"files_touched"`
	Agent        string      `json:"agent"`
	TurnID       string      `json:"turn_id"`
	TokenUsage   TokenUsage  `json:"token_usage"`
	Attribution  Attribution `json:"initial_attribution"`
}

type Checkpoint struct {
	Metadata
	Session SessionMetadata
	Prompt  string
	Context string
	Diff    string
	Plan    string
}

type CheckpointSummary struct {
	ID            string
	CreatedAt     time.Time
	Branch        string
	Agent         string
	AgentPercent  int
	CommitHash    string
	CommitMessage string
	Author        string
	Context       string
	Prompt        string
	TotalTokens   int
	Duration      string
	FileCount     int
	Additions     int
	Deletions     int
	SessionCount  int
}

type TranscriptEntry struct {
	Type      string
	Role      string
	Content   string
	ToolName  string
	Timestamp time.Time
	Tokens    int
}

	switch msg := msg.(type) {

From the action menu (press `a` in the TUI):

| Key | Command | What it does |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| `s` | `entire status --detailed` | Show entire.io state |
| `e` | `entire explain -c <id>` | AI analysis of checkpoint (requires checkpoint selected) |
| `d` | `entire doctor` | Diagnose stuck sessions |
| `w` | `entire rewind --to <id>` | Rewind to checkpoint (requires checkpoint, confirms first) |
| `m` | `entire resume <branch>` | Resume session on branch (requires checkpoint, confirms first) |
| `x` | `entire reset` | Delete session state (confirms first) |
| `c` | `entire clean` | Remove orphaned data (confirms first) |

**Status** captures output and shows it in a scrollable overlay. All others suspend the TUI and run interactively in the terminal.

**Checkpoint-specific** commands (`explain`, `rewind`, `resume`) are only available from the detail view — they're dimmed/disabled on the overview screen. **Destructive** commands (`rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, `clean`) prompt for y/n confirmation before executing.

input.prompt: Extract the VHS tape file syntax: all available commands (Set, Type, Sleep, Enter, Key, etc.), their parameters, and configuration options (Output, Width, Height, FontSize, Theme, etc.). Give me a complete reference of the tape language.

content.content:
# VHS Tape Language Reference

## Output & Requirements

**Output**
Specifies file location and format (GIF, MP4, WebM, or PNG sequence directory).
```
Output demo.gif
Output frames/
```

**Require**
Declares dependencies; must appear at the top before non-setting commands.
```
Require gum
Require glow
```

## Configuration Settings

Settings must be defined at the tape's beginning. `TypingSpeed` can be applied later.

| Command | Purpose | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `Set Shell` | Terminal shell | `Set Shell fish` |
| `Set FontSize` | Font size in pixels | `Set FontSize 46` |
| `Set FontFamily` | Font typeface | `Set FontFamily "Monoflow"` |
| `Set Width` | Terminal width in pixels | `Set Width 1200` |
| `Set Height` | Terminal height in pixels | `Set Height 600` |
| `Set LetterSpacing` | Character spacing in pixels | `Set LetterSpacing 20` |
| `Set LineHeight` | Line spacing multiplier | `Set LineHeight 1.8` |
| `Set TypingSpeed` | Delay per character | `Set TypingSpeed 500ms` |
| `Set Theme` | Color scheme (JSON or by name) | `Set Theme "Catppuccin Frappe"` |
| `Set Padding` | Frame padding in pixels | `Set Padding 0` |
| `Set Margin` | Video margin in pixels | `Set Margin 60` |
| `Set MarginFill` | Margin background color | `Set MarginFill "#6B50FF"` |
| `Set WindowBar` | Window decoration style | `Set WindowBar Colorful` |
| `Set BorderRadius` | Window corner rounding | `Set BorderRadius 10` |
| `Set Framerate` | Capture rate | `Set Framerate 60` |
| `Set PlaybackSpeed` | Playback speed multiplier | `Set PlaybackSpeed 2.0` |
| `Set LoopOffset` | GIF loop starting point | `Set LoopOffset 50%` |
| `Set CursorBlink` | Cursor animation toggle | `Set CursorBlink false` |

## Typing & Text Input

**Type**
Emulates keyboard input. Escape quotes with backticks.
```
Type "echo hello"
Type@500ms "Slow typing"
Type `VAR="Value"`
```

**Copy / Paste**
Clipboard operations.
```
Copy "https://example.com"
Paste
```

## Keyboard Commands

All key commands accept optional `@<time>` and repeat `count` parameters.

| Command | Function | Example |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `Backspace` | Delete characters | `Backspace 18` |
| `Enter` | Execute/confirm | `Enter 2` |
| `Tab` | Tab completion | `Tab@500ms 2` |
| `Space` | Space bar | `Space 10` |
| `Up` / `Down` / `Left` / `Right` | Arrow navigation | `Up 2` |
| `PageUp` / `PageDown` | Page scrolling | `PageUp 3` |
| `ScrollUp` / `ScrollDown` | Viewport scrolling | `ScrollDown@100ms 12` |
| `Ctrl+<key>` | Control sequences | `Ctrl+R` |

Modifiers stackable: `Ctrl+Alt+Shift+<char>`

## Timing & Synchronization

**Sleep**
Capture frames without interaction.
```
Sleep 500ms
Sleep 2
Sleep 1s
```

**Wait**
Pause until screen condition matches regex; default scope is `Line`.
```
Wait                    # Waits for prompt
Wait /pattern/          # Match pattern
Wait+Screen /pattern/   # Search entire screen
Wait+Line /pattern/     # Search last line only
Wait@10ms /pattern/     # Custom timeout interval
```

## Frame Control

**Hide / Show**
Toggle frame capture for setup/cleanup without recording.
```
Hide
Type "setup command"
Enter
Show
```

**Screenshot**
Capture current frame as PNG.
```
Screenshot examples/frame.png
```

## Advanced Features

**Env**
Set environment variables.
```
Env HELLO "WORLD"
```

**Source**
Include commands from another tape file.
```
Source config.tape
```

---

VHS requires `ttyd` and `ffmpeg` as system dependencies for operation.

content.text:
Created `demo.tape`. The demo flow:

1. Launch the TUI, pause on overview
2. `?` — show help screen (highlights new `a` binding)
3. `a` — open action menu on overview (checkpoint-specific items dimmed)
4. `j/k` — navigate through all 7 actions
5. `Esc` — close menu
6. `j` + `Enter` — select a checkpoint, enter detail view
7. `a` — action menu on detail (all items enabled)
8. `w` — select rewind, shows confirmation dialog
9. `n` — cancel confirmation
10. `a` + `s` — select status, shows captured output overlay
11. `Esc` — close output, `q` — quit

Run it with:
```
vhs demo.tape
```

**Note:** Requires checkpoint data in the repo (`entire/checkpoints/v1` branch). If the `entire` CLI isn't installed, the menu will show the "CLI not found" warning with all items disabled — still a valid demo of the UI, just less interactive.

  publish     Publish your GIF to vhs.charm.sh and get a shareable URL
  record      Create a new tape file by recording your actions
  serve       Start the VHS SSH server
  themes      List all the available themes, one per line
  validate    Validate a glob file path and parses all the files to ensure they are valid without running them.

Flags:
  -h, --help             help for vhs
  -o, --output strings   file name(s) of video output
  -p, --publish          publish your GIF to vhs.charm.sh and get a shareable URL
  -q, --quiet            quiet do not log messages. If publish flag is provided, it will log shareable URL
  -v, --version          version for vhs

Use "vhs [command] --help" for more information about a command.

content.text:
Here's your thread:

---

**1/8 (opener)**

👋 Just added all the entire.io CLI commands to entire-local's TUI. Press `a` and you get an action menu — navigate, select, execute. Like having the full CLI but without leaving the dashboard.

🧵 Let me walk you through each one ↓

---

**2/8 — Status**

`status` — The quick health check. Runs `entire status --detailed` and captures the output right in the TUI. No terminal switching, just read and scroll. The one command you'll use ten times a day without thinking about it.

---

**3/8 — Explain**

`explain` — AI-powered analysis of a checkpoint. Select a checkpoint, hit explain, and entire.io breaks down what happened in that session. Suspends the TUI, gives the CLI full control, comes back when it's done.

---

**4/8 — Doctor**

`doctor` — Something feels off but you can't tell what? Doctor diagnoses stuck sessions and offers fixes. Fully interactive — the TUI steps aside and lets you work through it in the terminal.

---

**5/8 — Rewind**

`rewind` — Go back in time. Navigate to a checkpoint, select rewind, and it rolls your working tree back to that point. This one's destructive so you get a confirmation dialog first. Cancel with `n`, commit with `y`.

---

**6/8 — Resume**

`resume` — Pick up where you left off. Select a checkpoint, hit resume, and it switches you to that branch to continue the session. Confirmation required since it changes your branch.

---

**7/8 — Reset**

`reset` — Clean slate. Deletes the current session state when things go sideways. Destructive, so it asks you to confirm before doing anything. Sometimes you just need to start over.

---

**8/8 — Clean**

`clean` — The janitor. Removes orphaned checkpoint data that's no longer referenced. Keeps your repo tidy. Confirmation required because once it's gone, it's gone.

---

Attach each GIF (`demo/menu.gif`, `demo/status.gif`, etc.) to its matching tweet.

# Plan: Add entire.io CLI commands to the TUI (approved)

## Context
The entire.io CLI has commands like `status`, `explain`, `doctor`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, and `clean`. entire-local currently only reads checkpoint data. We want to mirror these commands in the TUI by shelling out to the `entire` CLI binary, making entire-local a full interface for entire.io.

## UX Design: Action Menu

Triggered by pressing `a` (matching the existing pattern of single-key modes like `/` for filter, `?` for help). Shows a centered overlay box (same pattern as `help.go`).

**Menu items:**
```
Actions
─────────────────────────────
 [s]  Status       Show entire.io state
 [e]  Explain      AI analysis of checkpoint
 [d]  Doctor       Diagnose stuck sessions
 [w]  Rewind       Rewind to checkpoint
 [m]  Resume       Resume session on branch
 [x]  Reset        Delete session state
 [c]  Clean        Remove orphaned data
─────────────────────────────
 esc  Close
```

- Checkpoint-specific items (`explain`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`) are dimmed/hidden on the overview screen
- All items disabled with warning when `entire` CLI is not installed
- Navigate with `j/k`, select with `Enter` or shortcut key

## Execution Strategy

| Command | Mode | Confirmation | Notes |
|---------|------|-------------|-------|
| `status` | Capture output, show in overlay | No | `entire status --detailed` |
| `explain` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | `entire explain -c <id>` -- may stream/generate |
| `doctor` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | Interactive (offers fix options) |
| `rewind` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire rewind --to <commit>` -- destructive |
| `resume` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire resume <branch>` -- changes branch |
| `reset` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire reset` -- destructive |
| `clean` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire clean` -- destructive |

- **`tea.ExecProcess`**: Suspends TUI, runs command in real terminal, resumes TUI on exit. Good for interactive/long-running/destructive commands.
- **Capture**: Runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()` in a `tea.Cmd`, shows result in a scrollable overlay. Good for quick read-only output.
- After any write command completes, refresh checkpoint data via `m.loadCheckpoints()`.

## Confirmation Flow

For destructive commands (`rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, `clean`):
1. User selects action from menu
2. Confirmation overlay appears: `"Rewind to checkpoint abc1234? This will reset your working tree. (y/n)"`
3. `y` proceeds, `n`/`Esc` cancels

## Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/actions.go` | Action menu: item definitions, rendering, navigation, `buildEntireArgs()` |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/confirm.go` | Confirmation dialog: rendering, y/n handling |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/command_output.go` | Scrollable overlay for captured command output (used by `status`) |

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `dashboard.go` | Add fields to `model` (`showActions`, `actionCursor`, `entireCLIAvailable`, `confirmAction`, `commandOutput`, `showCommandOutput`). Wire overlays into `Update()` priority chain and `View()`. Add `detectEntireCLI()` to `Init()` via `tea.Batch`. |
| `keys.go` | Add `Actions` (`a`), `Confirm` (`y`), `Deny` (`n`) key bindings |
| `messages.go` | Add `entireCLIDetectedMsg`, `entireCmdFinishedMsg`, `entireCmdOutputMsg` |
| `styles.go` | Add `warningStyle`, `disabledStyle`, `actionSepStyle` |
| `help.go` | Add `a` -> `"Actions menu"` to help bindings |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: New message types and CLI detection
- Add msg types to `messages.go`
- Add `detectEntireCLI() tea.Cmd` using `exec.LookPath("entire")` in a new file `actions.go`
- Wire detection into `model.Init()` with `tea.Batch(m.loadCheckpoints(), detectEntireCLI())`
- Handle `entireCLIDetectedMsg` in `Update()`

### Step 2: Action menu definitions and rendering (`actions.go`)
- Define `entireAction` enum and `actionItem` struct (key, label, desc, requiresCheckpoint, destructive, execProcess)
- Define the 7 action items as a package-level slice
- Render function following `help.go` centered-box pattern
- Navigation: j/k cursor, Enter/shortcut-key select, Esc close
- `buildEntireArgs(action, detail)` maps actions to CLI args

### Step 3: Confirmation dialog (`confirm.go`)
- Render centered box with warning text and `y/n` prompt
- `handleConfirmKeys`: `y` -> execute, `n`/`Esc` -> cancel
- Confirmation messages per action (describing consequence)

### Step 4: Command output overlay (`command_output.go`)
- Scrollable panel for `status` output
- j/k scroll, Esc to close
- Reuses existing scroll patterns from detail view

### Step 5: Wire into dashboard.go
- Add new model fields
- `Update()` priority chain: commandOutput > confirm > actions > filtering > normal
- `View()` overlay priority: commandOutput > confirm > actions > help > normal screen
- Action execution:
  - ExecProcess commands: `tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("entire", args...), callback)`
  - Capture commands: `tea.Cmd` that runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()`
- Post-command: refresh checkpoints, clear state

### Step 6: Keys, styles, help updates
- Add key bindings in `keys.go`
- Add styles in `styles.go` (reuse existing colors: warning=accent/214, disabled=very dim)
- Add `a` action to help screen
- Update footer hints in both overview and detail screens

## Key Patterns to Follow
- `help.go` centered overlay box pattern for action menu, confirm, and output
- `messages.go` simple struct msg types
- `keys.go` `key.NewBinding()` pattern
- No comments inside function bodies
- Warm orange theme (208/214 palette)
- `exec.Command` pattern from `pkg/git/git.go`

## Verification
- `make build && make test && make lint` pass
- Press `a` on overview: see global commands, checkpoint-specific items dimmed
- Press `a` on detail: see all commands enabled
- Select `status`: overlay shows `entire status --detailed` output
- Select `explain`: TUI suspends, `entire explain` runs, TUI resumes
- Select `rewind` on detail: confirmation dialog, then `entire rewind` runs
- When `entire` not installed: all items disabled with warning

1→# entire-local
     2→
     3→[![CI](https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     4→[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)
     5→[![Go Reference](https://pkg.go.dev/badge/github.com/jcleira/entire-local.svg)](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)
     6→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
     7→
     8→A terminal UI for browsing [entire.io](https://entire.io) checkpoints locally. Zero network, zero auth -- just git.
     9→
    10→![demo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0332285d-f32f-4ea9-afcb-ede757b905d9)
    11→
    12→## Why
    13→
    14→entire.io stores checkpoint data in a local git orphan branch. The web dashboard requires granting read access to your GitHub repos. **entire-local** is a local-first alternative that reads that data directly from your repo, keeping everything offline and private.
    15→
    16→## Prerequisites
    17→
    18→- **Go 1.24+**
    19→- **git**
    20→- A repository with [entire.io](https://entire.io) enabled (the orphan branch must exist locally)
    21→
    22→## Installation
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→go install github.com/jcleira/entire-local@latest
    26→```
    27→
    28→Or build from source:
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→git clone https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local.git
    32→cd entire-local
    33→make install
    34→```
    35→

# entire-local

[![CI](https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)
[![Go Reference](https://pkg.go.dev/badge/github.com/jcleira/entire-local.svg)](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jcleira/entire-local)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)

A terminal UI for browsing [entire.io](https://entire.io) checkpoints locally. Zero network, zero auth -- just git.

![demo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0332285d-f32f-4ea9-afcb-ede757b905d9)

## Why

entire.io stores checkpoint data in a local git orphan branch. The web dashboard requires granting read access to your GitHub repos. **entire-local** is a local-first alternative that reads that data directly from your repo, keeping everything offline and private.

## Prerequisites

- **Go 1.24+**
- **git**
- A repository with [entire.io](https://entire.io) enabled (the orphan branch must exist locally)

## Installation

```bash
go install github.com/jcleira/entire-local@latest
```

Or build from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/jcleira/entire-local.git
cd entire-local
make install
```

## Usage

Navigate to any git repository with entire.io enabled and run:

```bash
entire-local
```

### Screens

- **Overview** -- Aggregated stats across all checkpoints: total sessions, token usage, and duration breakdown.
- **Checkpoint List** -- Browse and filter all checkpoints by prompt text. Press `/` to search.
- **Checkpoint Detail** -- Drill into a single checkpoint with three tabs:
  - **Transcript** -- Full conversation with syntax-highlighted code blocks.
  - **Files** -- Diff view showing all file changes with syntax highlighting.
  - **Plan** -- Rendered markdown plan for the session.

### Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|---|---|
| `j/k` | Navigate / scroll |
| `Enter/l` | Select checkpoint |
| `Esc/h` | Go back |
| `/` | Filter checkpoints |
| `Tab` | Next tab (detail view) |
| `Shift+Tab` | Previous tab |
| `1-3` | Jump to tab |
| `r` | Refresh data |
| `?` | Toggle help |
| `q` | Quit |

## Project Layout

```
cmd/                          Cobra command (root)
pkg/git/
  git.go                      ExecGit, RepoRoot, BranchExists
  reader.go                   Reader (ls-tree, show)
pkg/checkpoint/
  types.go                    Checkpoint & session types
  loader.go                   Loader (walks orphan branch tree)
  jsonl.go                    JSONL parser, flexTimestamp
  stats.go                    Token/duration stats
pkg/ui/
  commands/
    messages.go               PrintError/PrintInfo helpers
  dashboard/
    dashboard.go              Bubble Tea model, Init, Update, View
    keys.go                   Key bindings
    messages.go               Msg types
    styles.go                 Lipgloss styles
    overview.go               Overview screen
    checkpoint_list.go        Checkpoint list screen
    checkpoint_detail.go      Checkpoint detail (Files, Plan tabs)
    transcript.go             Transcript tab
    markdown.go               Markdown renderer
    help.go                   Help screen
```

## Development

```bash
make build     # Build binary
make test      # Run tests
make lint      # Run golangci-lint
make check     # fmt + vet + lint
make deps      # Download & tidy dependencies
```

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines.

## License

[MIT](LICENSE)

# Plan: Add entire.io CLI commands to the TUI (approved)

## Context
The entire.io CLI has commands like `status`, `explain`, `doctor`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, and `clean`. entire-local currently only reads checkpoint data. We want to mirror these commands in the TUI by shelling out to the `entire` CLI binary, making entire-local a full interface for entire.io.

## UX Design: Action Menu

Triggered by pressing `a` (matching the existing pattern of single-key modes like `/` for filter, `?` for help). Shows a centered overlay box (same pattern as `help.go`).

**Menu items:**
```
Actions
─────────────────────────────
 [s]  Status       Show entire.io state
 [e]  Explain      AI analysis of checkpoint
 [d]  Doctor       Diagnose stuck sessions
 [w]  Rewind       Rewind to checkpoint
 [m]  Resume       Resume session on branch
 [x]  Reset        Delete session state
 [c]  Clean        Remove orphaned data
─────────────────────────────
 esc  Close
```

- Checkpoint-specific items (`explain`, `rewind`, `resume`, `reset`) are dimmed/hidden on the overview screen
- All items disabled with warning when `entire` CLI is not installed
- Navigate with `j/k`, select with `Enter` or shortcut key

## Execution Strategy

| Command | Mode | Confirmation | Notes |
|---------|------|-------------|-------|
| `status` | Capture output, show in overlay | No | `entire status --detailed` |
| `explain` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | `entire explain -c <id>` -- may stream/generate |
| `doctor` | `tea.ExecProcess` | No | Interactive (offers fix options) |
| `rewind` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire rewind --to <commit>` -- destructive |
| `resume` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire resume <branch>` -- changes branch |
| `reset` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire reset` -- destructive |
| `clean` | `tea.ExecProcess` | Yes | `entire clean` -- destructive |

- **`tea.ExecProcess`**: Suspends TUI, runs command in real terminal, resumes TUI on exit. Good for interactive/long-running/destructive commands.
- **Capture**: Runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()` in a `tea.Cmd`, shows result in a scrollable overlay. Good for quick read-only output.
- After any write command completes, refresh checkpoint data via `m.loadCheckpoints()`.

## Confirmation Flow

For destructive commands (`rewind`, `resume`, `reset`, `clean`):
1. User selects action from menu
2. Confirmation overlay appears: `"Rewind to checkpoint abc1234? This will reset your working tree. (y/n)"`
3. `y` proceeds, `n`/`Esc` cancels

## Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/actions.go` | Action menu: item definitions, rendering, navigation, `buildEntireArgs()` |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/confirm.go` | Confirmation dialog: rendering, y/n handling |
| `pkg/ui/dashboard/command_output.go` | Scrollable overlay for captured command output (used by `status`) |

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `dashboard.go` | Add fields to `model` (`showActions`, `actionCursor`, `entireCLIAvailable`, `confirmAction`, `commandOutput`, `showCommandOutput`). Wire overlays into `Update()` priority chain and `View()`. Add `detectEntireCLI()` to `Init()` via `tea.Batch`. |
| `keys.go` | Add `Actions` (`a`), `Confirm` (`y`), `Deny` (`n`) key bindings |
| `messages.go` | Add `entireCLIDetectedMsg`, `entireCmdFinishedMsg`, `entireCmdOutputMsg` |
| `styles.go` | Add `warningStyle`, `disabledStyle`, `actionSepStyle` |
| `help.go` | Add `a` -> `"Actions menu"` to help bindings |

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: New message types and CLI detection
- Add msg types to `messages.go`
- Add `detectEntireCLI() tea.Cmd` using `exec.LookPath("entire")` in a new file `actions.go`
- Wire detection into `model.Init()` with `tea.Batch(m.loadCheckpoints(), detectEntireCLI())`
- Handle `entireCLIDetectedMsg` in `Update()`

### Step 2: Action menu definitions and rendering (`actions.go`)
- Define `entireAction` enum and `actionItem` struct (key, label, desc, requiresCheckpoint, destructive, execProcess)
- Define the 7 action items as a package-level slice
- Render function following `help.go` centered-box pattern
- Navigation: j/k cursor, Enter/shortcut-key select, Esc close
- `buildEntireArgs(action, detail)` maps actions to CLI args

### Step 3: Confirmation dialog (`confirm.go`)
- Render centered box with warning text and `y/n` prompt
- `handleConfirmKeys`: `y` -> execute, `n`/`Esc` -> cancel
- Confirmation messages per action (describing consequence)

### Step 4: Command output overlay (`command_output.go`)
- Scrollable panel for `status` output
- j/k scroll, Esc to close
- Reuses existing scroll patterns from detail view

### Step 5: Wire into dashboard.go
- Add new model fields
- `Update()` priority chain: commandOutput > confirm > actions > filtering > normal
- `View()` overlay priority: commandOutput > confirm > actions > help > normal screen
- Action execution:
  - ExecProcess commands: `tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("entire", args...), callback)`
  - Capture commands: `tea.Cmd` that runs `exec.Command().CombinedOutput()`
- Post-command: refresh checkpoints, clear state

### Step 6: Keys, styles, help updates
- Add key bindings in `keys.go`
- Add styles in `styles.go` (reuse existing colors: warning=accent/214, disabled=very dim)
- Add `a` action to help screen
- Update footer hints in both overview and detail screens

## Key Patterns to Follow
- `help.go` centered overlay box pattern for action menu, confirm, and output
- `messages.go` simple struct msg types
- `keys.go` `key.NewBinding()` pattern
- No comments inside function bodies
- Warm orange theme (208/214 palette)
- `exec.Command` pattern from `pkg/git/git.go`

## Verification
- `make build && make test && make lint` pass
- Press `a` on overview: see global commands, checkpoint-specific items dimmed
- Press `a` on detail: see all commands enabled
- Select `status`: overlay shows `entire status --detailed` output
- Select `explain`: TUI suspends, `entire explain` runs, TUI resumes
- Select `rewind` on detail: confirmation dialog, then `entire rewind` runs
- When `entire` not installed: all items disabled with warning
